title: Dactylorhiza × variabilis
intro: Dactylorhiza × variabilis, a hybrid orchid species, is a type of terrestrial orchid commonly found in various parts of the world. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including cardiovascular problems, diabetes, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits of lowering blood sugar levels and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents responsible for its medicinal properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory activities. Dactylorhiza × variabilis is often used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × variabilis is a hybrid species of orchid. It is a cross between Dactylorhiza fuchsii and another species within the Dactylorhiza genus. This orchid hybrid is classified within the family Orchidaceae. Dactylorhiza × variabilis is a hybrid of terrestrial orchids, commonly found in the UK and other parts of Europe. The classification of Dactylorhiza × variabilis reflects its unique genetic makeup and characteristics.
